justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Auth per consumer type correctly designed — JWT for mobile, API key for partners with distinct validation paths; (2) Internal traffic separation fully addressed with dedicated internal mesh on separate network plane, explicit network segmentation, and clear statement that internal traffic bypasses the gateway; (3) Business logic explicitly excluded — repeated in component table, pitfalls section, and auth-vs-authorization distinction; (4) SPOF risk identified as Critical severity with stateless replicas + L4 LB mitigation; (5) Webhook delivery for partners addressed with dedicated Webhook Dispatcher service, async event bus, exponential retry, and dead-letter queue; (6) Both flexibility_points and rigidity_points present with detailed tables; (7) Technology recommendation includes Kong, Envoy, and AWS API Gateway with build/buy consideration tied to team expertise.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough separation of concerns — clearly distinguishes authentication (gateway) from authorization (services), which is a nuance many designs miss
Strangler Fig migration strategy is practical and reduces deployment risk, with phased rollout plan
Webhook architecture is well-designed: async via message bus, HMAC signing, exponential backoff, dead-letter queue, and partner dashboard visibility
Rate limiting design uses token bucket with Redis Lua scripts for atomic operations — addresses distributed consistency correctly
Rigidity points are genuine constraints (network segmentation, auth-at-edge-only, pipeline ordering) rather than restating flexibility points in reverse
Redis fail-open policy for rate limiting shows mature operational thinking — availability over enforcement
Gateway latency per hop (1-5ms typical) not explicitly quantified in trade-offs, though Redis latency (~0.5ms) is mentioned
Vendor lock-in risk for managed gateway services not called out as a standalone risk, though technology alternatives are discussed
Header spoofing mitigation (stripping X-Consumer-* from incoming requests) shows security depth beyond the basic requirements

agent_output_excerpt:
## Executive Summary

The logistics platform needs a unified API Gateway layer that centralizes cross-cutting concerns (authentication, rate limiting, routing) while maintaining strict traffic isolation between three consumer tiers. The architecture uses a single gateway deployment with three logical ingress paths, an auth pipeline pattern, and a plugin-based rate limiting strategy — avoiding the complexity of three separate gateways while preserving isolation guarantees.
